# Pocket Gull

> Insight beneath the surface. A voice-first clinical co-pilot for AI-augmented patient intake and care plan synthesis.

Pocket Gull is an Angular v21.1 progressive web application deployed on Google Cloud Run. It combines a Three.js interactive 3D anatomical body map with Google Gemini AI (`gemini-2.5-flash` via `@google/adk` multi-agent orchestration) and the Web Speech API to help clinicians rapidly synthesize patient intake data into structured care plan recommendations.

This application was built for the Gemini Live Agent Challenge hackathon (Category: Live Agents — Real-time Voice & Visual UI).

- Live URL: https://pocket-gall-444980566010.us-west1.run.app
- Repository: https://github.com/philgear/pocket-gull
- Developer: https://g.dev/philgear

## Key Capabilities

- **Live AI Consult:** Interruptible voice-first conversational co-pilot powered by the Web Speech API and `@google/adk` LlmAgent orchestration.
- **Care Plan Recommendation Engine:** Generates structured clinical strategies across four diagnostic lenses: Overview, Interventions, Monitoring, and Education.
- **Interactive 3D Body Mapping:** Three.js skeletal/surface model for precise anatomical symptom annotation.
- **Interactive Task Bracketing:** Double-click state machine (Normal → Added → Removed) for physician vetting of AI recommendations.
- **Patient Management System:** Full CRUD for patient records with FHIR Bundle import/export and local auto-save.
- **Printable Clinical Stationery:** CSS Grid `@media print` layouts with Halftone body maps.
- **Research Integrations:** Google Programmable Search Engine (CSE) and NIH PubMed E-utilities for auxiliary real-time clinical context.
- **Box Breathing UX:** 16-second box breathing animations in intake fields to promote practitioner mindfulness.
- **Smartwatch Support:** Responsive layouts down to 286px (Pixel Watch 2).

## Technology Stack

- **Framework:** Angular v21.1 — Signals-based, Zoneless, with SSR and Client-Side Hydration
- **Visualization:** Three.js v0.183
- **AI/ML:** Google GenAI SDK (`gemini-2.5-flash`), Google Agent Development Kit (`@google/adk`), Web Speech API
- **Backend:** Express.js (Node.js) — proxies PubMed E-utilities, serves static search integration
- **Styling:** Tailwind CSS, Dieter Rams-inspired design system
- **Export:** jsPDF, FHIR R4 Bundle standard (Unicode-safe Base64)
- **Infrastructure:** Google Cloud Run, Docker, Cloud Build

## Architecture

Angular frontend (SSR, Zoneless Signals) → Express.js backend on Cloud Run.
PatientState service manages reactive application state via Angular Signals.
ADK InMemoryRunner orchestrates specialized LlmAgents, each targeting a clinical lens, streaming partial JSON back to the Analysis component.
Backend proxies `/api/pubmed` to NCBI E-utilities (XML→JSON) and statically serves `search.html` for Google CSE iframe postMessage integration.
All patient data persists in browser localStorage only. No remote patient database exists.

## API Endpoints (Backend)

- `GET /api/pubmed?query=<term>` — Proxies NCBI PubMed E-utilities and returns structured article results as JSON.
- `GET /search.html` — Serves the Google Programmable Search Engine integration page.
- `GET /*` — Serves the Angular SSR-rendered application.

## Responsible AI

Pocket Gull is a clinical productivity tool, not a medical device. All AI-generated content requires explicit physician review and approval via Task Bracketing before clinical use. Patient data is processed transiently; no PII is persisted remotely or used for model training. The platform is designed to augment, not replace, licensed clinical judgment.

## Data Handling

- All patient session data (intake, brackets, history) stored in browser `localStorage` only.
- AI inference calls transmit only the current patient context snapshot to `gemini-2.5-flash` via transient HTTPS requests.
- No remote database. No analytics tracking of patient data. FHIR R4 compliant export.
- Gemini API key is managed as a Cloud Run environment variable; never exposed to the client.
